title: Add create_schematic operation
description: The volta has no operation to create a new .kicad_sch file. All 19+ operations require an existing target file. Add a create_schematic op that generates a valid empty KiCad schematic with proper header, UUID, paper size, and title block. Also consider create_pcb and create_project variants. Schema is in src/volta/ops/schema.py, executor in src/volta/ops/executor.py.

title: Add create_symbol operation for new component definitions
description: The volta has no operation to create new component symbols in .kicad_sym library files. AddLibEntryOp only adds library references to sym-lib-table. Need a create_symbol op that defines a symbol with name, pins (number, name, type, position), graphic elements, and properties (reference, value, footprint, datasheet). This is needed to add new ICs like the AK4619VN codec that don't exist in standard or project libraries.

title: Contextual Placement Constraints
description:
PCB schematics capture electrical connectivity but NOT physical placement intent. Experienced designers carry implicit knowledge ('regulator can\'t be near analog', 'connector must be on edge', 'decoupling cap within 5mm'). volta has ZERO infrastructure for contextual placement rules.

MISSING:
- Per-component edge/region affinity (J1 must be on board edge)
- Relational proximity (U3 must avoid U1 within 25mm — EMI)
- Relational approach (C4 within 5mm of U3 — decoupling)
- Orientation/facing (LED1 faces up — mechanical)
- Named-region assignment (analog section is left half)

PROPOSED: PlacementRule constraint model
- rule_type: edge_affinity | avoid | approach | orientation | region
- Sources: explicit, inferred, learned, imported
- Enforcement: SA objective, placement gate, routing keepouts
- Persistence: constraints.json sidecar, each rule carries rationale for AI

Related: SchGen (Microsoft 2026), PCBSchemaGen (2026), Phase 104 BlockerDiagnostician, SKIDL integration

title: Phase 108 D-4: Arduino_Mega .kicad_pro file corruption (double extension)
description: tests/fixtures/Arduino_Mega/ has Arduino_Mega.kicad_pro.kicad_pro (double extension — git-state artifact) instead of Arduino_Mega.kicad_pro. KiCad 10 requires the project file to load the schematic; kicad-cli sch export pdf returns 'Failed to load schematic' for BOTH original and autolaid output. Pre-existing fixture infrastructure issue discovered during Phase 108 Task 2 visual verification. Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET per four-state taxonomy. Fix: rename file. Readiness signal: this Bead exists; Phase 108 Task 2 on-page guarantee verified via 2 small-fixture PDFs + geometry gate.
notes: PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION: Restored correct Arduino_Mega.kicad_pro filename (was double-extension). Removed stale lock files. HOWEVER kicad-cli still cannot load schematic due to 129 malformed R? symbols — that deeper corruption split out as separate Bead. The .kicad_pro rename portion is DONE; symbol corruption tracked separately.

title: Phase 108 D-5: _parse_symbol_refs no-rotation regex bug
description: src/volta/schematic_routing/schematic_graph.py:594 _parse_symbol_refs regex requires (at X Y R) — three-number rotation. Symbols without explicit rotation (like Arduino_Mega J1 at (at 100.0 200.0)) are invisible to the topology builder. Pre-existing bug discovered during Phase 108 Task 2 — the on-page parking logic works around it by scanning raw content directly for (symbol (lib_id ...)) blocks, but the underlying parser bug should be fixed. Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET. Fix: change regex to make rotation optional: r'\(symbol\s+\(lib_id\s+"([^"]+)"\)\s+\(at\s+[\d.]+\s+[\d.]+(?:\s+[\d.-]+)?\)'. Affects: any code consuming SchematicGraph.ref_to_libid for symbols without rotation.

title: Phase 108 D-6: SugiyamaLayout Y-axis inversion documented but not implemented
description: src/volta/schematic_autolayout/sugiyama.py line 11 docstring claims 'Schematic Y is INVERTED (handled in stage 5 coordinate assignment)' but assign_coordinates does NOT implement any inversion — Y grows positive-downward with layer index (y_raw = layer * layer_spacing). Pre-existing gap discovered during Phase 108 Task 2 source review. Doesnt affect on-page guarantee (the layout still fits the page) but the docstring is inaccurate and the visual orientation may not match KiCad eeschema conventions for some boards. Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET. Fix: either implement the inversion (subtract from page_height) or correct the docstring. Investigate whether KiCad coordinate system actually requires inversion for schematic layout (it does for PCB plot but schematic Y may be non-inverted).

title: Arduino_Mega fixture: 129 malformed R? symbols (missing instances, empty Value)
description: Discovered while fixing D-4 (kicad_pro rename). Even after restoring the correct .kicad_pro filename, kicad-cli sch export pdf returns 'Failed to load schematic' for Arduino_Mega. Root cause: 129 of 143 placed symbols are malformed R? resistors: (1) missing (instances ...) blocks (KiCad 10 requires instances on every placed symbol), (2) empty (property "Value" "") fields, (3) all stacked at (at 50 30). The corruption grew across commits: 6 R? at 5c11d905, 84 at d4591b42, 129 now. The autolayout on-page fix (Phase 108 Task 2) correctly relocates these via UUID-based parking so the SCHEMATIC GEOMETRY is sound (off_page=0, max_stack=1), but KiCad 10.0.3 still refuses to LOAD the file. Fix requires: regenerate Arduino_Mega.kicad_sch from the original Arduino source, OR programmatically repair the 129 malformed symbols (add instances blocks, populate Value, distribute positions). Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET. Priority P2 — blocks visual PDF verification of the medium-fixture class.

title: Document all operations in prompt.md - 12+ missing
description: prompt.md only documents 19 operations but the schema has 31+. Missing: AddWireOp, AddLabelOp, AddPowerOp, AddJunctionOp, AddNoConnectOp, AddNetClassOp, AssignNetClassOp, AddCopperZoneOp, AddDesignRuleOp, AutoRouteOp, RepairSchematicOp, SetBoardOutlineOp, ValidatePowerNetsOp. These all work in the schema but are not documented for LLM consumption.

title: Add component search and symbol import MCP server
description: volta needs a way to import component symbols from external sources (SnapMagic, Ultra Librarian, LCSC, Octopart). Currently there's no create_symbol operation and no way to fetch existing symbols from part databases. Build an MCP server that: (1) searches for parts by name/MPN, (2) downloads KiCad symbol + footprint into project library, (3) returns pinout/spec data. This would eliminate the manual symbol creation workaround and speed up schematic design across all 22+ modules. Priority sources: SnapMagic (free KiCad symbols), LCSC (already sourcing from), Octopart (aggregator API).

title: Manually created AK4619VN symbol - workaround for missing create_symbol op
description: Workaround: manually created AK4619VN KiCad symbol in shared library because volta has no create_symbol operation (volta-2) and no component import MCP (volta-4). Also manually created pair-card.kicad_sch because no create_schematic operation (volta-1). Future: automate via create_symbol op + SnapMagic/LCSC MCP.

title: add_design_note op — annotate schematics with reason, intention, and math
description:
## Problem
volta has no operation for placing design-intent annotations on a schematic. The only text-bearing primitives are net labels, reference designators, and symbol values — none of which capture WHY a value was chosen, WHAT a subcircuit is for, or HOW a target was derived. The result is schematics that are electrically correct but cognitively opaque. A reviewer or future designer has to reverse-engineer intent from component values.

## Inspiration
Bart Instruments' open-source DUAL SSI2130 VCO CORE (Rev 3.0, 2026-07-07) demonstrates the pattern beautifully. Their schematic embeds inline annotations that turn a pile of resistors into a legible design:

  - "10uA/oct"              (states the SSI2130 expo converter constant)
  - "target 47.17uA"        (states the design goal for the scale trim current)
  - "2.5/55000=45.5 [uA] + 5/1000000=5 [uA]"  (derives the result from resistor values)
  - "0-5V CV mixed to 2.5V"  (states intention of the CV summing node)
  - "EXPONENTIAL CONVERTER CVs" / "HF TRIM" / "THROUGH-ZERO FM AND PM"  (block headers)
  - "EXAMPLE PWM CV CONNECTION"  (callout for reference subcircuit)

These are ordinary KiCad `(text ...)` elements with multi-line content (\n in string), positioned near the relevant circuitry. The schematic reads like a design doc.

## Proposed op
Add `add_design_note` to the readability schema family (alongside `review_schematic`). Suggested shape:

```
{
  "op": "add_design_note",
  "target_file": "hardware/foo/foo.kicad_sch",
  "text": "2.5/55k = 45.5uA\n5/1M = 5uA\ntarget: 50.5uA at EXPO_FREQ (10uA/oct)",
  "at": [45.97, 69.85]                  # absolute position in mm
  "anchor": {                            # OR anchored to a component/net
    "ref": "U4",
    "side": "above-left",
    "offset": [5.0, 5.0]
  },
  "kind": "formula",                     # formula | intention | assumption | target | block-header | reference
  "font_size": 1.27,                     # default 1.27 (KiCad default)
  "justify": "left bottom",
  "bold": false
}
```

### Op variants to consider
1. `add_design_note` — single multi-line text element (this request)
2. `add_text_block` — KiCad `(text_box ...)` with border, title, and size for longer rationale
3. `annotate_component` — wraps `add_design_note` with auto-positioning relative to a refdes (avoids manual coordinate wrangling)
4. `annotate_net` — links the note to a net name so it stays attached during moves

## Why this matters
Schematics without annotations are write-only. The analog-ecosystem project has 55+ modules — without embedded rationale, every review, every refactor, every handoff requires re-deriving the design intent from scratch. Annotations convert schematics from netlists into documents.

Reference: https://github.com/bartinstruments/open-source/tree/main/DUAL%20SSI2130%20VCO%20CORE

## Implementation hints
- Schema: src/volta/ops/_schema_readability.py (alongside ReviewSchematicOp)
- Executor: add op_add_design_note() that emits a `(text "..." (at X Y) (effects ...))` S-expression
- Multi-line: KiCad text accepts \n in the string literal; effects justify applies per line
- No ERC impact: text elements are non-electrical, exclude_from_sim yes
- Position helper: reuse coordinate logic from net_label_placer.py for anchor-relative placement

title: Per-VCO measurement tap for digital autotune
description:
## Pattern
Buffer a square-wave tap from each VCO into a comparator, route the comparator output to an MCU capture input. Firmware measures period and computes pitch correction current, which is injected via DAC into the SCALE_TRIM or TUNE_TRIM node.

## Why
Without a measurement tap, oscillator drift is uncorrectable in software. Discrete VCOs (synth-machine) drift with temperature, supply voltage, and aging. AS3394E (synth-voice) drifts less but still benefits from calibration tables. The measurement tap is the difference between an instrument that stays in tune and one that doesn't.

## Where to apply
- **synth-machine** (8 voices): CRITICAL. Discrete MMBT3904 expo converters have no on-chip compensation.   Tap the existing comparator output that generates the square wave, buffer it, route to a spare ADC channel on the RP2350.
- **synth-voice** (1 voice, AS3394E): Useful. AS3394E has a square output already; just route it to an ADC input.

## Implementation
- **Use a comparator, not an op-amp buffer.** LM393 (~$0.10) has open-collector output and clean edge transitions.   Op-amp square waves have slow edges that hurt period measurement accuracy.
- **Pull-up resistor on LM393 output** (10kΩ to 3.3V) for the open-collector stage.
- **MCU timer input capture** peripheral gives period measurement with sub-microsecond resolution.
- **Calibration curve stored in flash** — measure pitch error at C2, C4, C6, build a 256-entry lookup table.

## Reference
Bart Instruments DUAL SSI2130 VCO CORE Rev 3.0 (2026-07-07):
- U9: LM393 dual comparator → 1_MEASURE_OUT and 2_MEASURE_OUT nets
- SSI2130 has dedicated SQUARE_RAW pin (pin 8 per datasheet) feeding the comparator
- https://github.com/bartinstruments/open-source/tree/main/DUAL%20SSI2130%20VCO%20CORE

## Related
- Depends on synth-machine / synth-voice having a spare ADC channel (verify in firmware pin mapping)
- Pairs with the SCALE_TRIM / TUNE_TRIM DAC current injection (see related bead)

title: HF tracking trim network for discrete VCO cores
description:
## Pattern
Add a high-frequency compensation network to discrete saw-core expo converters. Without it, the oscillator tracks flat (slow) at high audio frequencies (C5-C8 range, ~1kHz-8kHz).

## Why
The exponential converter's theoretical response assumes ideal transistor behavior. Real transistors have: finite base resistance, transit time, and storage capacitance — all of which cause the high-frequency timing current to lag. The CEM3340 / SSI2130 / AS3394E have this compensation built into the IC. Discrete MMBT3904 pairs do not.

The SSI2130 actually exposes a feedback loop:
- Pin 6 (HF_TRACK, OUTPUT) — the IC reports its own high-frequency tracking state
- Pin 20 (HFT_BASE, INPUT) — conditioned signal returns
This is closed-loop compensation: the chip participates in its own correction.

Discrete VCOs can't do this directly — there's no chip reporting its own state. The discrete equivalent is a feedforward network: a tempco resistor + small signal diode (1N4148) that progressively boosts timing current at higher frequencies. The measurement tap (related bead) closes the loop in firmware.

## Where to apply
- **synth-machine** (8 voices): REQUIRED. Without HF trim, the 8-voice polyphonic synth will be audibly   out of tune at upper-register notes.
- **synth-voice** (AS3394E): N/A — handled on-chip.

## Implementation
- Place a 2kΩ tempco (TN0402B2001F) in place of the standard resistor in the expo converter emitter
- Add a 1N4148 across the timing capacitor to clip the top of the ramp at high frequencies
- Add a 10-50pF cap in parallel with the timing cap to inject a small HF-boost current
- The Bart Instruments HF TRIM block (around U2/U7 op-amps in their schematic) shows the SSI2130-specific implementation

## Reference
Bart Instruments DUAL SSI2130 VCO CORE Rev 3.0:
- HF TRIM block labeled explicitly in schematic (positions around X=279, Y=21 and X=68, Y=21)
- Note: SSI2130's HF_TRACK feedback pin has no direct discrete equivalent
- https://github.com/bartinstruments/open-source/tree/main/DUAL%20SSI2130%20VCO%20CORE

## Related
- Pairs with measurement tap (volta-30) — firmware autotune handles what HF trim can't
- Engineering reference: 'Analog Synthesizer Design' by Douglas Self, chapter on VCO tracking

title: Local ±2.5V references per voice submodule (not inverter-derived)
description:
## Pattern
Place two separate LM4040-2.5 precision voltage references on each voice submodule — one for +2.5V, one for -2.5V. Do NOT generate the negative rail by inverting the positive.

## Why
Pitch accuracy depends directly on the CV reference voltage. If +2.5V drifts by 1%, every note drifts by 1%. Two independent references give matched tolerance and tempco — the rails track each other thermally. A single reference + inverter introduces an op-amp offset and tempco mismatch between rails.

Carrier/backplane IR drop destroys tracking on long signal paths. Local references per voice card isolate pitch accuracy from backplane variation — the reference lives millimeters from the expo converter.

## Where to apply
- **synth-voice** (AS3394E): Already has DACs and ADC for control, but no dedicated ±2.5V references. ADD.
- **synth-machine cartridge**: ADD. Each cartridge gets its own pair.
- **Any voice card that produces pitch CV** — applies broadly.

## Implementation
- 2× LM4040DBZ-2.5 (SOT-23, ~$0.40 each) per voice card
- +2.5V reference biased from carrier +12V via 10kΩ resistor
- -2.5V reference biased from carrier -12V via 10kΩ resistor
- 100nF decoupling cap on each reference output
- Place references adjacent to the expo converter on the PCB (thermal coupling)

## Reference
Bart Instruments DUAL SSI2130 VCO CORE Rev 3.0:
- U11: LM4040DBZ-2.5 (positioned at 83.82, 257.81 — near power section)
- U12: LM4040DBZ-2.5 (positioned at 64.77, 264.16 — adjacent to U11)
- Power section also includes TPS70950 LDO for local +5V (Lead: separate takeaway for noise rejection)
- https://github.com/bartinstruments/open-source/tree/main/DUAL%20SSI2130%20VCO%20CORE

## Related
- Complements local LDO pattern (TPS70950, also in Bart design)
- Required before measurement tap (volta-30) is useful — DAC pitch correction needs stable reference

title: Passive-summed multiple V/oct inputs (eliminate buffer op-amps)
description:
## Pattern
Multiple pitch CV sources summed passively at the expo converter input via resistors. Each source contributes current to the summing node per Ohm's law (I = V/R). Eliminates the buffer op-amp stage that would otherwise be needed for each CV input.

## Why
Standard approach: each CV input → buffer op-amp → summing op-amp → expo converter. This wastes op-amp channels (2-3 per VCO) and adds offset/error per stage.

Bart's approach: each CV input → series resistor → common summing node at the expo converter pin. No op-amps. The expo converter's virtual ground at the summing node does the work.

Three V/oct inputs cost 3 resistors. Three V/oct inputs buffered cost 3 op-amps + 3 resistors + summing amp. Passive summing is cheaper, simpler, lower noise.

## Where to apply
- **synth-machine** (8 voices × 3 V/oct inputs each): Replace current MCP4728 + op-amp topology.   Each voice sums root-note DAC + pitch-bend CV + fine-tune CV via 100kΩ resistors.
- **synth-voice** (AS3394E): AS3394E has a single V/oct pin, but multiple CV sources can still sum passively.

## Implementation
- Calculate resistor values from the expo converter's per-octave current requirement
- SSI2130 wants ~10µA per octave at the EXPO_FREQ pin (Bart's annotation: '10uA/oct')
- 1V/octave input → R = V/I = 1V/10µA = 100kΩ
- Fine tune input (0-5V = 1 octave range) → R = 5V/10µA = 500kΩ (use 499kΩ 1%)
- Scale trim input (0-3.3V = ±0.5 octave range) → R = 3.3V/5µA = 680kΩ
- Each input contributes independently; total current at summing node is I_total = Σ(V_i / R_i)

## Math (from Bart schematic)
Bart's SCALE_TRIM derivation, copied from their inline annotation:
  2.5V / 55000Ω = 45.5µA  (bulk scale current from +2.5V reference)
  5V / 1000000Ω = 5µA     (fine scale current from +5V via 1MΩ)
  total: 50.5µA at EXPO_FREQ (target: 47.17µA per their note — slight overhead for trim range)

## Reference
Bart Instruments DUAL SSI2130 VCO CORE Rev 3.0:
- 'EXPONENTIAL CONVERTER CVs' block (2 copies, one per VCO)
- 3× V/OCT_1, V/OCT_2, V/OCT_3 inputs (each via 100kΩ)
- FINE_TUNE input (0-5V range, ~1 octave)
- SCALE_TRIM input (0-3.3V range, MCU DAC)
- TUNE_TRIM input (0-5V range, MCU DAC)
- HFT_TRIM input (HF compensation)
- All summed into SSI2130 EXPO_FREQ pin 7 via resistive network
- https://github.com/bartinstruments/open-source/tree/main/DUAL%20SSI2130%20VCO%20CORE

## Related
- Depends on local ±2.5V references (volta-32) for the reference rails
- Pairs with measurement tap (volta-30) for closed-loop autotune
